video:video.video:title: Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh on return of 'Deli Boys' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh are back for more "Deli Boys" as the comedy begins streaming its second season on Hulu and Disney+.

video:video.video:title: Thalía set free with ’Todo Suena Mejor en Cumbia’ | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Thalía says "everything sounds better in cumbia." The Latin pop star's 17th studio album "Todo Suena Mejor en Cumbia" features original songs and covers.

video:video.video:title: Spencer Pratt holds at primary night party in Los Angeles
video:video.video:description: Spencer Pratt holds at primary night party in Los Angeles

video:video.video:title: Steven Spielberg: 'Disclosure Day' isn't science fiction | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Steven Spielberg's “Disclosure Day" returns Hollywood’s preeminent big-screen craftsman to one of his most abiding questions: Are we alone?

video:video.video:title: Packers’ Micah Parsons offers more conservative update regarding his potential return
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers edge rusher Micah Parsons discusses a possible timeline for his return from a torn left anterior cruciate ligament that ended his 2025 season prematurely.

video:video.video:title: Supreme leader's message urges Iranians to 'stand firm' as they mark Khomeini death anniversary
video:video.video:description: Iran's supreme leader warned that the country’s enemies, after what he described as battlefield setbacks, are pursuing “hybrid warfare” aimed at weakening public resilience and influencing government decision-making.

video:video.video:title: Putin answers series of questions from international media alongside St Petersburg Economic Forum
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin held a meeting with the heads of international news agencies on Thursday and said that Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside the country.

video:video.video:title: Retired president Raúl Castro turns 95 as Cuba celebrates enigmatic figure
video:video.video:description: Raúl Castro has turned 95, a landmark birthday for a man still helping lead one of the last communist countries in the world.

video:video.video:title: 'Disclosure Day' cast invades London
video:video.video:description: Steven Spielberg and the cast of "Disclosure Day" land in London for the sci-fi epic's UK premiere.

video:video.video:title: After escaping the Taliban and years in exile, Afghanistan women's soccer team rises again
video:video.video:description: The Afghanistan soccer federation doesn’t recognise the women’s team which played its last official competitive match in 2018.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures severe winds collapsing a large tent in West Virginia University ball park
video:video.video:description: Powerful winds tossed a large white tent at a college baseball game in West Virginia in a burst of severe weather that injured five people, according to officials with West Virginia University.

video:video.video:title: Balkan leaders attend EU summit in Montenegro as enlargement gains urgency
video:video.video:description: The Balkans are seen as crucial in countering security and economic threats from Russia and China. High on the agenda was Montenegro's potential EU accession.

video:video.video:title: ICE detainee's daughter describes medical neglect in immigration detention centers
video:video.video:description: An investigation by The AP and KFF Health News found immigrant detainees in 33 states alleged the U.S. is failing to provide them with adequate medical care.

video:video.video:title: Authorities say a man barricaded himself inside a bank in California along with several others
video:video.video:description: Police say a man barricaded himself inside a bank building in the Southern California city of Bakersfield with an unknown number of people.

video:video.video:title: FIFA opens international broadcast center ahead of 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: FIFA officially opened its International Broadcast Center in Dallas on Monday, unveiling the facility that will serve as the broadcast and technology hub for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Rubio testifies before Congress for the first time since the start of the Iran war
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio faced a litany of questions about the Trump administration’s fragile or stalling diplomatic efforts around the world.

video:video.video:title: Brunson thanks his teammates after Knicks take Game 1 of the NBA Finals
video:video.video:description: Jalen Brunson praised his teammates after the Knicks took Game 1 of the NBA Finals — but said the team is staying focused on what comes next.

video:video.video:title: Top Republican hopeful for California governor Steve Hilton reacts to primary results
video:video.video:description: Steve Hilton, a former Fox News host and top Republican contender for California governor, doubled down on a message he kept revisiting throughout his campaign as he waited for ballots to be tallied: (AP Video by Terry Chea)

video:video.video:title: Stocks slump as Big Tech sinks and a strong May jobs report boosts odds for higher interest rates
video:video.video:description: The U.S. stock market had its worst day since October as a sell-off in big technology companies weighed down the broader market.

video:video.video:title: Kuwait releases surveillance video of deadly drone strike on its international airport
video:video.video:description: Footage from Kuwait's Directorate General of Civil Aviation showed the moment of impact from several angles.

video:video.video:title: About 500,000 people pour into a Madrid plaza for an evening prayer vigil with Pope Leo XIV
video:video.video:description: About 500,000 people, many of them young Spaniards, poured into a Madrid plaza for an evening prayer vigil with Pope Leo XIV on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Macron and Kagame attend inauguration of memorial to Rwanda's Tutsi genocide in Paris
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron and Rwandan President Paul Kagame on Tuesday inaugurated a monument in Paris in memory of the victims of the Rwanda genocide.

video:video.video:title: Head of UN mission in DRCongo visits Bunia to address response to Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: James Swan, who heads the nearly 9,000-member U.N. peacekeeping force in Congo known as MONUSCO, said he wanted to make sure UN personnel were respecting health protocols.

video:video.video:title: Beijing's E-Town home to dozens of robotic research, development and manufacturing companies
video:video.video:description: The robots stand ready to demonstrate their capabilities – driving screws to assemble component, sorting out delivery packages, or dancing to entertain human visitors.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia bets on AI personal computers with new chip powering Windows laptops
video:video.video:description: Nvidia unveiled on Monday new powerful chips that would bring advanced artificial intelligence functions into laptops and desktop computers, with the new personal computer models from brands including Microsoft and Dell set to roll out later this year.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO throws ceremonial first pitch at S.Korean baseball game
video:video.video:description: Nvidia founder and CEO Jensen Huang threw the ceremonial first pitch at a South Korean baseball game in Seoul.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 3
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, June 3rd: Iranian attack on Kuwait’s airport reported; Massive aerial attack in Ukraine; California holds primary in race for Governor; L.A. mayor goes to runoff in reelection bid.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 2
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 2nd: Massive Russian attack across Ukraine; Trump says Israel and Hezbollah to dial back fighting; Senate Republicans want answers about Trump settlement fund; Shooting suspect and six others dead in Iowa town.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Tirana over a coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner
video:video.video:description: Protest in Tirana over a coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner

video:video.video:title: Chase Infiniti hopes 'The Testaments' inspires young women
video:video.video:description: Chase Infiniti says "The Testaments" has "a chance to make some change." She won outstanding lead performance in a drama series at the Gotham TV Awards.

video:video.video:title: 'Office Romance' star Jennifer Lopez has not soured on workplace relationships
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Lopez says even if past relationships have not worked out, she hasn't closed the door on meeting future love interests at work. (June 5)

video:video.video:title: Watch: LA mayor candidate Spencer Pratt says he hopes incumbent Karen Bass is ‘ready’
video:video.video:description: “We can do debates every Friday if she would like,” Pratt told a group of reporters under the flash of cameras in his first remarks after results started rolling in.

video:video.video:title: US military posts video of a strike on an alleged drug boat, killing 2 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military has attacked another boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two men.

video:video.video:title: Tony nominee Caissie Levy says 'Ragtime' feels especially timely
video:video.video:description: Tony nominee Caissie Levy says 'Ragtime' is connecting with audiences because its story helps people make sense of the emotions and challenges of the current moment.

video:video.video:title: Fuel shortages leave Havana streets piled with garbage
video:video.video:description: Cubans already struggling with constant power outages are dealing with piles of trash pilling up in the streets. (AP/ Ariel Fernandez)

video:video.video:title: Candles lit in windows of US consulate in Hong Kong on Tiananmen crackdown anniversary
video:video.video:description: Lit candles lined the windows of the U.S. Consulate in Hong Kong on Thursday to commemorate the deadly 1989 military crackdown on student-led pro-democracy protests in China's Tiananmen Square.

video:video.video:title: Philippines' President Marcos and Japan's PM Takaichi sing 'Imagine' and 'Hey Jude' during state dinner
video:video.video:description: Philippines President Ferdinand Marcos Jr and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi were seen singing along to hits, including 'Hey Jude' by The Beatles and 'Imagine' by John Lennon during a state dinner in Tokyo on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Giving birth on a dirt road: conflict makes childbirth risky in parts of Africa
video:video.video:description: Women are 40 times more likely to die in pregnancy or childbirth in Central African Republic than in the United States, according to the United Nations.

video:video.video:title: Chuck D remembers Peabo Bryson
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of Questlove's "Earth, Wind, and Fire" documentary, Chuck D reflects on the impact of singer Peabo Bryson. (June 3)

video:video.video:title: Armenians vote in general election under Russian pressure aimed at preventing a drift toward West
video:video.video:description: Armenians voted in a parliamentary election on Sunday as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, sought to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian maritime drone explodes at a Romanian Black Sea port
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian maritime drone that was being used in the country's war against Russia exploded Friday at a Black Sea port in Romania, while three other sea drones exploded outside the port, Romanian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh want to keep South Asian representation on screen
video:video.video:description: "Deli Boys" actors Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh reflect on the bigger goal behind the Hulu and Disney+ series.

video:video.video:title: Pro-Trump candidate pulls ahead in Colombia presidential vote as ruling party sows doubt in results
video:video.video:description: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race in the first round of voting Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of Colombia’s outgoing President Gustavo Petro who questioned the results of the election.

video:video.video:title: Spielberg says 'Disclosure Day' is too real to be sci-fi
video:video.video:description: Nearly 50 years after making "Close Encounters of the Third Kind," Steven Spielberg returns to the question of extraterrestrial life with "Disclosure Day." (June 3)

video:video.video:title: French navy intercepts Russia-linked oil tanker in the Atlantic
video:video.video:description: The French Navy, with support from the United Kingdom, has intercepted an oil tanker under international sanctions that was traveling from Russia, the latest effort by nations that support Ukraine to target Russian oil exports helping to finance President Vladimir Putin’s war.

video:video.video:title: House passes bill to aid Ukraine and impose new sanctions on Russia
video:video.video:description: The House has passed a bill to aid Ukraine and sanction key segments of the Russian economy. The vote Thursday could generate the House's second major foreign policy break this week with President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Pope honours Spain's tradition of religious devotion as a 'school of faith' during Madrid mass
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV honoured Spain's centuries-old tradition of religious devotion on Sunday during a Mass before a million people.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 1
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, June 1st: Protesters demonstrate against Ebola quarantine center for Americans in Kenya; Demonstrations across Israel against mandatory enlistment in Israel's military; Florida sues OpenAI, claiming the company and its CEO concealed risks to the public; Peonies bloom an

video:video.video:title: AP captures dramatic drone visuals of Sagrada Familia days away from arrival of Pope Leo XIV
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is expected to visit Barcelona next week and give a blessing for the iconic Sagrada Familia's Tower of Jesus Christ that made the basilica the world’s tallest church when it was raised in October.

video:video.video:title: Residents killed and injured in devastating fire at nursing home in Sri Lanka
video:video.video:description: A fire at a nursing home in western Sri Lanka killed 12 residents and injured eight others, police said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Prime minister and Serbian opposition leader vote in Kosovo elections
video:video.video:description: Kosovo's Prime Minister cast his ballot on Sunday as voters headed to the polls for an early parliamentary election, the third in less than 18 months, in an attempt to unlock a political impasse in the small Balkan nation.

video:video.video:title: Putin says Russia will bolster its air defenses in response to Ukrainian drone attacks
video:video.video:description: In response to a question from The Associated Press during a meeting with heads of international news agencies, the Russian president also said his country is open for a compromise on Ukraine in line with understandings reached during his summit with President Trump in Alaska.

video:video.video:title: Sánchez, Fujimori vote in runoff election as EU observers deploy across Peru
video:video.video:description: Peruvian presidential candidates Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori voted in Lima on Sunday as the nation went to the polls to decide their ninth president in just 10 years.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim showcases new warship ahead of visit by China's Xi
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un observed sea trials of a new warship repaired after a failed launch last year and vowed to accelerate efforts to build a nuclear-armed navy, state media said Saturday, showcasing his expanding military capabilities ahead of a visit by Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief reports 5 Ebola recoveries as a new treatment center opens in eastern Congo
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization has reported five patients have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus in eastern Congo.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 5th: UN force clears rubble in Lebanon town; Ukrainians react positively to Zelenskyy's letter to Putin; EU leaders meet to discuss the Balkans; so-called Leonardo's ferry helps river crossing in Italy.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV departs for Spain for a 7-day Apostolic visit
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV departed for Spain on Saturday for a 7-day Apostolic visit. Leo is expected to double down on his messages of unity amid polarization, peace as war rages, welcome for migrants and hope for young Spaniards in the era of artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Steampunk festival brings Victorian style and sci-fi oddity to New Zealand town
video:video.video:description: Steam-powered vehicles and Victorian costumes have taken over the New Zealand town of Ōamaru for its annual Steampunk Festival.

video:video.video:title: New York says it is prepared for World Cup events
video:video.video:description: New York says it is prepared for the upcoming World Cup games and will deploy additional law enforcement to aid in the patrolling of major transit hubs and other special events.

video:video.video:description: A Sherpa guide was found crawling to base camp on Mount Everest a week after he went missing, and was reunited with his family who had given up hope he would return.

video:video.video:title: Police investigate Iowa man suspected of shooting 6 of his relatives and then himself
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Iowa are investigating the fatal shootings of six people who they believe were killed by a relative who took his own life when confronted by police Monday.

video:video.video:description: Fairfax County prosecutor speaks after Brendan Banfield was sentenced to life in prison without parole for the murder of his wife and a man who was lured to the couple’s home as a fall guy.

video:video.video:description: Israel struck Iran on Monday after being targeted by missiles, while a U.S. military base in Saudi Arabia came under fire in the most serious exchange of hostilities since an April ceasefire, raising the possibility of a return to heavy fighting and complicating mediation efforts to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Why Leo's first encyclical went viral and how it speaks to his papal approach
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has issued a manifesto calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence. His first encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” has sparked widespread online reactions.

video:video.video:description: Various rumors and mistrust have persisted in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo as the region battles an Ebola outbreak. (AP Video by Justin Kabumba)

video:video.video:description: The upcoming Colombian presidential election between Sen. Iván Cepeda, an ally of President Gustavo Petro, and lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella, whom U.S.

video:video.video:title: 'We'll sell to someone else': Lula defiant after Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil
video:video.video:description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva remained defiant on Tuesday after the Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil.

video:video.video:description: A series of bizarre sightings of people popping in and out of New York’s vast, subterranean sewer system in recent days has the whole city wondering what exactly is going on.

video:video.video:title: Attorney Ben Crump calls for upgraded charges in North Carolina police assault case
video:video.video:description: A doorbell camera video captures former City of Shelby Police Department officer Karson Hyder repeatedly punching Cherrie Moore in the face.

video:video.video:title: Residents flee Beirut's southern suburbs after Israel orders strikes
video:video.video:description: Traffic jams were seen in Southern Beirut, Monday, as residents attempted to flee after Israel ordered strikes on Dahiyeh where Hezbollah enjoys widespread support.

video:video.video:title: Residents flee Beirut's southern suburbs after Israel orders strikes
video:video.video:description: Traffic jams were seen in Southern Beirut, Monday, as residents attempted to flee after Israel ordered strikes on Dahiyeh where Hezbollah enjoys widespread support.

video:video.video:title: Trump on fate of anti-weaponization fund: 'I don't know'
video:video.video:description: In his first public comments since his Justice Department said a controversial $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund would not go forward, the president equivocated.

video:video.video:title: A century since birth of Hollywood icon Marilyn Monroe
video:video.video:description: It's been a century since the birth of Hollywood icon Marilyn Monroe on June 1, 1926.

video:video.video:title: Michelle Pfeiffer says she learned 'OnlyFans' can be 'all kinds of things'
video:video.video:description: Michelle Pfeiffer, who stars in 'Margo's Got Money Troubles,' says the show helped her learn more about the online platform.

video:video.video:title: Peruvians are soon to choose their president, but neither candidate has convinced the electorate
video:video.video:description: Peru has two options when they head to the polls on Sunday: Keiko Fujimori of the Fuerza Popular political party and Roberto Sánchez of the Juntos por el Perú political party, who, out of more than 30 presidential candidates in the first round, were the top two choices.

video:video.video:title: Activists protest in Abuja, demand rescue of abducted schoolchildren
video:video.video:description: Nigerian activists and civil society campaigners on Thursday staged a protest in Abuja demanding the rescue of pupils and teachers abducted from schools in Oyo State, as concerns grow over recurring kidnappings targeting educational institutions across the country.

video:video.video:title: The Nigerian army frees 360 abducted people in northeastern Borno state
video:video.video:description: The Nigerian army said Sunday it freed 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in southern Borno, in the northeastern part of the country.

video:video.video:title: DHS secretary praises state cooperation at New Jersey ICE facility
video:video.video:description: Markwayne Mullin says if needed, he has a plan to pull CBP officers to help with security at the Delaney Hall facility, but says the state is working to provide security there so it’s not needed right now.

video:video.video:title: Lonnie Ali reflects on Muhammad Ali's legacy of compassion 10 years after his death
video:video.video:description: The Ali Center in Muhammad Ali's hometown of Louisville, Kentucky, is sponsoring a “Day of Compassion” this week to promote acts of service and caring.

video:video.video:title: Steven Spielberg is convinced there's alien life
video:video.video:description: As Steven Spielberg returns with new sci-fi movie "Disclosure Day," the director explains why his long-held belief in alien life has turned into a conviction. (June 3)

video:video.video:title: Senate passes $70B immigration bill enforcement bill in new funding for ICE, Border Patrol
video:video.video:description: The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump’s immigration enforcement agencies early Friday, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.

video:video.video:title: Jerome Powell strongly defends Fed independence while accepting JFK Profile in Courage Award
video:video.video:description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell used one of his first major public appearances since stepping down as chairman to strongly defend the independence of the central bank, saying that could destroy "priceless" credibility.

video:video.video:title: Deadly Israeli strikes reported in southern Lebanon as tensions remain high
video:video.video:description: Deadly Israeli strikes were reported in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, as tensions remain high despite U.S.

video:video.video:title: Are wired headphones cool again? We asked AP staffers
video:video.video:description: Wired headphones are having a moment again, with some people embracing them for their reliability, sound quality and freedom from battery anxiety.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Ye holds concert in the Dutch city of Arnhem despite opposition from Jewish groups
video:video.video:description: A concert by rapper Ye, formerly Kanye West, took place in the eastern Dutch city of Arnhem on Saturday despite vocal opposition from Jewish groups and many Dutch lawmakers.

video:video.video:title: P!NK's Tony Awards plan is simple: 'Don't screw up'
video:video.video:description: P!NK is raising her glass to a new challenge. The Grammy-winning performer is stepping into the spotlight as host of the 79th Annual Tony Awards.

video:video.video:title: Business in the front, party in the back - Danish championships celebrates much-maligned mullet
video:video.video:description: A packed Danish crowd Saturday celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle at the country’s annual Mullet Championships. (AP Video by James Brooks)

video:video.video:title: AP exclusive: Iran's soccer players share their thoughts on the war and the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Iran's national football team intensified their preparations on Wednesday ahead of FIFA World Cup, holding an energetic training session in Antalya, Turkey as excitement builds across their country ahead of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Police respond to scene where multiple people were shot near a festival in Toledo, Ohio
video:video.video:description: Multiple people were shot Saturday afternoon near a community street festival in Toledo, Ohio, police said. Officers are actively searching for suspects.

video:video.video:title: Iranian football team arrives in Mexico for World Cup preparations
video:video.video:description: The Iranian football team arrived in Mexico amid high security early on Sunday for its World Cup preparations.

video:video.video:title: Kelli O'Hara enjoys looseness of 'Fallen Angels'
video:video.video:description: Kelli O'Hara and Rose Byrne co-starred in the revival of "Fallen Angels" on Broadway. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Atlantic hurricane season forecast to be milder than normal thanks to El Nino
video:video.video:description: Meteorologists predict a developing El Nino could dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won't eliminate storms.

video:video.video:title: Everyday life appears normal in Tehran, but Iran's central bank says inflation at WWII levels
video:video.video:description: Life appeared normal in Tehran on Tuesday, while the country's central banks says inflation has reached a level unseen since World War II, amid a shaky ceasefire with the United States and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Authorities say one person is dead and another injured after a train strikes a semi-truck in Iowa
video:video.video:description: A train has struck a semi-truck in eastern Iowa, killing one person and injuring another. Poweshiek County Sheriff Matt Maschmann said one person in the truck died, while another with serious injuries was taken to a hospital.

video:video.video:title: Skepticism on both sides as Lebanon-Israel ceasefire is extended
video:video.video:description: The U.S.-brokered agreement, announced in a joint statement by the U.S., Israel and Lebanon Wednesday, comes after Israeli forces made their deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter century.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians suffer from lack of proper toilets across Gaza's vast tent cities
video:video.video:description: Palestinians have been suffering from lack of proper toilets across Gaza's vast tent cities. In their bare-bones tent in southern Gaza, Mostafa Shaaban built his family’s makeshift toilet behind a curtain in a corner.

video:video.video:title: Mental fitness exam ordered for man charged with murder in three 'heinous' killings in Hawaii
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors say the killings of three men on Hawaii's Big Island were “especially heinous, atrocious, or cruel.”

video:video.video:title: Small plane crash leaves four people dead in Croatia
video:video.video:description: Croatian police say that a small plane crashed, killing four people. The plane came down on Thursday near Medulin, a small town on the Istria peninsula, a statement said.

video:video.video:title: Flamingos signal hope amid restoration efforts in Venice's fragile lagoon
video:video.video:description: Beyond Venice’s crowded canals is a fragile lagoon ecosystem. The scientists and environmental groups working to restore these wetlands say a sharp rise in the number of flamingos may be a sign their efforts are paying off.

video:video.video:title: Constance Zimmer is glad 'Love Story' brought back '90s nostalgia
video:video.video:description: Constance Zimmer, who stars as Carolyn Bessette's mother in the TV series 'Love Story,' says she's glad 'that side of the story was represented.' (June 1)

video:video.video:title: Researchers in Chile hope for further funding for their hantavirus treatment trials
video:video.video:description: When a rare but deadly rodent-borne virus struck passengers on a cruise ship and seemed to be spreading, there were no treatments for those who fell ill and no vaccines to protect others.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election, with the ruling party expected to win
video:video.video:description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party.

video:video.video:title: Javier Bardem highlights humor of new 'Cape Fear'
video:video.video:description: Oscar-winning actor Javier Bardem takes up the mantle of playing iconic villain Max Cady in new Apple TV series "Cape Fear," which follows film adaptations starring Robert Mitchum and Robert De Niro in the Cady role.

video:video.video:title: Funeral of baby killed by Israeli forces in West Bank
video:video.video:description: A funeral was held on Saturday after Israeli troops killed a seven-month-old Palestinian baby boy when they fired at his parents’ vehicle in the occupied West Bank, the Palestinian health ministry said.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the Chinese President visiting North Korea
video:video.video:description: Xi Jinping's trip will be the latest in a series of steps by China to reinforce its close ties with its nuclear-armed neighbor.

video:video.video:title: What happens when Tim Allen and Tom Hanks go out to eat?
video:video.video:description: "Toy Story" stars Tim Allen and Tom Hanks share funny interactions they've had with fans while dining together.

video:video.video:title: Thousands march in Jerusalem’s annual Pride parade amid tight security
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people march in Jerusalem’s annual Pride parade, an event tightly secured by police

video:video.video:title: Aid cuts deepen maternal health crisis for Sudan refugees in Central African Republic
video:video.video:description: In remote communities near the Sudan-Central African Republic border, pregnant women who fled Sudan’s war are struggling to access health care as clinics close and aid funding dries up.

video:video.video:title: Bear injures four in Fukushima
video:video.video:description: A bear has injured four people in a Japanese residential area in the latest case of attacks by the animals in the region. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Voters head to polls in Iowa
video:video.video:description: Iowa voters head to the polls to settle one of the Democrat party’s last competitive U.S. Senate primaries (AP Video by Hannah Fingerhut)

video:video.video:description: Speaking about his latest album, ‘Lottery,’ Shaggy says he creates music that stays true to himself rather than chasing trends, and believes that authenticity is what continues to resonate with audiences.

video:video.video:description: Actor Ewan McGregor says his role in the beloved film “Trainspotting” helped launch his career and set a tone for filmmaking.

video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday rejected a proposal by Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy for a face-to-face negotiation. Speaking at the St.

video:video.video:title: Lufthansa employees injured in Boeing nose gear incident at Frankfurt airport
video:video.video:description: A number of employees of German airline Lufthansa were injured after the nose gear of a stationary aircraft unexpectedly retracted at Frankfurt airport on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: 50 Cent credits director for success of 'Sean Combs: The Reckoning'
video:video.video:description: Executive producer 50 Cent wants to make it clear that director Alexandria Stapleton is behind the success of "Sean Combs: The Reckoning," the Netflix documentary series about Diddy's legal battles.

video:video.video:description: Tony Gilroy, the creator and director behind the acclaimed "Star Wars" spinoff "Andor," accepted a Peabody Award for the series’ second season and reflected on what he hopes to see from the franchise moving forward: “I like to think that they’ll stay ambitious.”

video:video.video:description: On a recent afternoon in Cuba, the temperature climbed and anxiety grew among the residents of a Havana street.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he wants his new acting director of national intelligence to slash positions in the office, which has already been significantly scaled back during his second term.

video:video.video:description: A day after U.S. President Donald Trump said Israel and Hezbollah agreed to dial back fighting, people in Beirut and northern Israel shared mixed reactions to the latest development.

video:video.video:title: Six people injured in Russian strike on Odesa
video:video.video:description: Six people were injured in an overnight Russian drone attack in Odesa, Maryna Averina, from the State Emergency Service of Odesa region reported on Monday.

video:video.video:title: The highest bridge in the world invigorates remote mountain communities in China
video:video.video:description: Building the Huajiang Grand Canyon Bridge in Guizhou province, has been a massive engineering project.

video:video.video:description: Air sirens went off across Kuwait City earlier on Monday as Tehran struck Kuwait in response to the United States bombing military sites in Iran.

video:video.video:description: Growing numbers of job applicants are no longer facing a human person in their first interview. Instead, candidates are confronted with AI chatbots designed to screen applications and assess answers before involving a human recruiter.

video:video.video:description: Hundreds of protesters took to the streets of Kenya’s capital on Monday, calling for the government to urgently investigate rising cases of gender-based violence and femicide, or the killing of women.

video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 2nd: President Trump signs executive order on artificial intelligence; Marco Rubio grilled on Capitol Hill; Iranian news reported Iran stopped communicating with mediators; Drug cartel tunnel discovered in California.

video:video.video:title: World Cup dream brings hope to Haiti amid violence and instability
video:video.video:description: Haiti’s qualification for the 2026 World Cup is offering many people across the country a rare sense of hope as the Caribbean nation struggles through one of the most difficult periods in its recent history.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian maritime drone explodes at a Romanian Black Sea port
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian maritime drone that was being used in the country’s war against Russia exploded Friday at a Black Sea port in Romania, while three other sea drones exploded outside the port, Romanian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Key takeaways from the high-stake races for California governor and LA mayor
video:video.video:description: The high-stakes races for California governor and LA mayor remained too close to call, but the battle lines for the November election are already being drawn as the candidates lay out their visions for leading the nation's most populous state.

video:video.video:description: Matt Groening and James L. Brooks celebrate the longevity of "The Simpsons," as producer Brooks is celebrated at the Peabody Awards.

video:video.video:title: A fish fight off Florida tests Trump’s drive to deregulate America’s seas
video:video.video:description: A federal judge in Washington has blocked a plan to loosen rules for fishing red snapper in the Atlantic, halting what was expected to be the longest recreational snapper season in years.

video:video.video:title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs days after US-supported ceasefire deal
video:video.video:description: Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs without warning on Sunday, days after a ceasefire agreement in Washington went into effect and despite a US request not to attack Lebanon's capital.

video:video.video:title: California GOP candidate Steve Hilton says ‘the argument for change is irresistible’
video:video.video:description: Republican candidate for California governor said on Tuesday he wanted to break the Democrats’ winning streak in the state. (AP video shot by: Eugene Garcia)

video:video.video:description: After a federal judge blocked US President Donald Trump’s planned renovation of the Kennedy Center, Trump on Friday said he's staying involved in efforts to overhaul the building.

video:video.video:description: Iowa’s waterways face significant pollution challenges due to agricultural runoff and sewage. Authorities say many lakes and streams do not meet standards for swimming, drinking or aquatic life.

video:video.video:description: Serena Williams is returning to professional tennis at age 44, accepting a wild card entry into a doubles tournament nearly four years after her last competitive match and fueling speculation about a possible Wimbledon comeback.

video:video.video:title: Sub-Saharan Africa is the deadliest place in the world to have a baby, while its population booms
video:video.video:description: Maternal mortality in sub-Saharan Africa, which has the world’s fastest-growing population, accounts for 70% of global maternal deaths.

video:video.video:description: The fatal stabbing of a British teenager who was handcuffed by police while his killer stood nearby erupted into a debate Tuesday about policing, race and knife crime.

video:video.video:description: Spanish hotel chain Meliá has joined a growing list of companies with a long-standing presence in Cuba that are withdrawing or limiting their presence on the island.

video:video.video:description: French Culture Minister Catherine Pegard described Wednesday the loaning of France's Bayeux Tapestry to Britain as a "political, cultural, and symbolic" act.

video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, June 7: Israel strikes Beirut days after ceasefire takes effect; Police search for suspects after Ohio festival shooting; Over 1 million see Pope Leo XIV in Madrid, Spain; Trump to attend NBA Finals Game 3 in New York.

video:video.video:title: Rubio warns AI could 'destabilize' societies as it reshapes jobs worldwide
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that while artificial intelligence will have some "very positive impacts," it could "destabilize societies all over the world" as it reshapes economies and eliminates jobs.

video:video.video:title: At least one Israeli killed in shooting attack in central Israel, police and paramedics say
video:video.video:description: A Palestinian man with Israeli citizenship has opened fire in central Israel, killing a reservist and wounding five other people.

video:video.video:title: Iran's national team prepares for World Cup as war continues
video:video.video:description: Iran's national football team intensified their preparations on Wednesday ahead of the FIFA World Cup, holding an energetic training session in Antalya, Turkey as excitement builds across their country ahead of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Soccer great Pelé's legacy shines on in his one-time home ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As the 2026 FIFA World Cup approaches, the image of one of Brazil's most recognizable soccer players lives on in Santos, the one-time home of Edson Arantes do Nascimento, far better known as Pelé.

video:video.video:title: Landmarks and businesses are preparing for the World Cup's economic impact in Atlanta
video:video.video:description: Atlanta is not only welcoming soccer fans during the World Cup, but also business opportunities. Georgia is building the biggest fan zone the city has seen since the Olympics, landmarks are investing in soccer-themed souvenirs, and small businesses are finding new ways to expand.

video:video.video:title: Wall of nametags at South Korean park captures adoptees’ longing for their birth mothers
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Korean adoptees from North America and Europe recently gathered to leave their names on a wall at a former U.S. military base, hoping that, after decades, a birth mother might still be looking for them.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds protest outside Kenya's Ebola quarantine center for US citizens
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of youths in Kenya’s central town of Nanyuki on Monday demonstrated against the establishment at the Laikipia Air Base of an Ebola quarantine center for American citizens exposed to the virus.

video:video.video:title: Putin defends state of Russian economy in meeting with international media
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday claims the Russian economy was struggling amid the ongoing military operation in Ukraine were "wishful thinking".

video:video.video:title: Five killed in explosion and fire at defense company in Daejeon, South Korea
video:video.video:description: An explosion and fire occurred at a defense company in South Korea on Monday, killing five people and injuring two others, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Daniel Radcliffe has no plans to stop doing theater
video:video.video:description: Daniel Radcliffe has been in six Broadway productions. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: UN forces clear rubble from Lebanese village of Dibbine after Israeli troop withdrawal
video:video.video:description: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon on Friday removed rubble and cleared the village of Dibbine, the first area from which Israeli troops withdrew from Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Gov. Greg Abbott lays out plans to combat a New World screwworm infestation in Texas
video:video.video:description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott lays out plans to combat a New World screwworm infestation in Texas, emphasizing that there is a well-established prevention and cure for the spread that the federal government has implemented in the past.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian President writes direct letter to Putin calling for negotiations to end the war
video:video.video:description: The letter is the first public message Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has written directly to Russian President Vladimir Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief honors four nurses who survived Ebola in DR Congo
video:video.video:description: The Director General of the World Health Organisation, Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, presented four nurses with certificates on Sunday confirming that they had recovered from Ebola in Bunia, eastern DRC.

video:video.video:title: Colorful displays show World Cup anticipation has reached city in Brazil's Amazon rainforest
video:video.video:description: As countries prepare for the FIFA World Cup, anticipation has reached fever pitch in a city in the heart of the Brazilian rainforest. (AP Video by Fernando Crispim)

video:video.video:title: Dozens of children separated under first Trump administration have faced separation again
video:video.video:description: An Associated Press investigation reveals that dozens of children who were separated under the first Trump administration have been re-separated, despite a judge's order to reunite them.

video:video.video:title: Virginia man gets life in prison for double murder scheme in affair with Brazilian au pair
video:video.video:description: Brendan Banfield who was having an affair with the family’s Brazilian au pair was sentenced Friday to life in prison without parole for the murder of his wife and a man who was lured to the couple's home as a fall guy.

video:video.video:title: US strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean in fourth attack this week
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it carried out another strike Saturday on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing three men in the fourth attack this week.

video:video.video:title: A new exchange of fire with Iran in the Gulf tests the fragile ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The latest exchange of fire came as the Trump administration pressed Iran to make a deal to end the war that has strained the global economy and threatened a hunger crisis in some of the world’s most vulnerable countries.

video:video.video:title: Fear grows ahead of Peru's runoff election as extortion and killings increase
video:video.video:description: Voters in Peru say violence and extortions are their main concerns as they head to the ballot box to elect a new president in a runoff elections.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Pulte won’t be the ‘permanent’ director of national intelligence
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says Bill Pulte’s appointment as acting director of national intelligence would only be temporary, praising him as “a very smart guy” while saying the White House is actively interviewing candidates for the Senate-confirmed role.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates art, culture and sport at Madrid cultural event
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV watched a twirling Flamenco dancer and received a badminton racket from two athletes at an event in Madrid honoring art, culture and sport.

video:video.video:title: Iranian missiles aimed at Israel seen over Middle Eastern sky
video:video.video:description: Missiles were seen over the middle eastern sky on Sunday after Israel said Iran has launched missiles at it in the first such bombardment since a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April, complicating mediation efforts for a deal to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Footballers in Rio de Janeiro favela find hope and opportunity from their sport ahead of World Cup
video:video.video:description: When Brazilian João Victor Gonçalves began playing soccer in one of Rio de Janeiro’s poorest and most violent favelas, little did he know the game would one day allow him to travel abroad and play in an international competition.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon considers its largest amnesty in 35 years but some are protesting
video:video.video:description: Lebanon's largest amnesty since the end of its devastating 1975-90 civil war is expected to occur in the coming weeks, once parliament approves it and the president signs it.

video:video.video:title: Video shows moment truck driver helps thwart alleged kidnapping
video:video.video:description: Footage from a truck-driving preacher's dashboard camera captured what appears to be a kidnapping attempt. Anthony J.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia bets on AI personal computers with new chip powering Windows laptops
video:video.video:description: Nvidia unveiled new powerful chips on Monday that would bring advanced artificial intelligence functions into laptops and desktop computers.

video:video.video:title: Scores of people rescued from deadly building fire in New Delhi
video:video.video:description: A fire swept through a building in a New Delhi neighborhood Wednesday, killing at least 18 people and injuring several others, news agency Press Trust of India reported.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 3
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, June 3rd: Trump called Netanyahu ‘crazy’, says Israel is complicating Iran talks; Ukrainian drones strike in St.

video:video.video:title: Shark fatally mauls spearfishing diver off Australia's Rottnest Island, police say
video:video.video:description: A shark fatally mauled a diver off an Australian tourist island on Saturday, police said.

video:video.video:title: Pope names Mexican-American broadcasting executive to lead Vatican communications
video:video.video:description: Maria Montserrat Alvarado replaces Paolo Ruffini as prefect of the Dicastery of Communications, the office that controls the Vatican’s television, radio, online, publishing and newspaper operations.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian cities of Dnipro and Kharkiv hit in overnight Russian drone and missile strikes
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Ukraine with a barrage of missiles and drones overnight, killing at least 11 people, injuring dozens and trapping others, authorities said on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Fear shadows Peru’s runoff vote as extortion and killings surge nationwide
video:video.video:description: Peru is grappling with a surge in extortion and violence, especially in areas like Trujillo. Gladys Saavedra, who works in a market there, says extortionists demand $300 monthly from vendors.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Lima as Peruvians choose between two presidential hopefuls with very different views
video:video.video:description: Peruvians cast their ballots on Sunday in an election that will allow voters to choose their ninth president in just 10 years, either the conservative daughter of a disgraced former president or a nationalist congressman.

video:video.video:title: Steve Hilton recalls Arnold Schwarzenegger's California governorship in speech
video:video.video:description: Republican Steve Hilton, a conservative commentator and former British political adviser, said he would give the liberal state a reset after years of one-party rule.

video:video.video:title: What to look forward to in the NBA finals
video:video.video:description: Game one of the NBA Finals is Wednesday, and AP reporter Tim Reynolds is there. Here's what to expect.

video:video.video:title: One dead and another injured after train strikes semi-truck in Iowa
video:video.video:description: A train struck a semi-truck in eastern Iowa on Wednesday, killing one person and injuring another, authorities said. (AP Video by Charlie Niebergall)

video:video.video:title: Smoke over southern Lebanese village as Israel continues its attacks against Hezbollah
video:video.video:description: Thick smoke billowed over the southern Lebanese village of Kfar Tebnit on Friday as Israel continues its attacks against Hezbollah.

video:video.video:title: Buffalo named Donald Trump for his golden locks delights crowds in Bangladesh
video:video.video:description: The rare albino bull became a sensation when a farmer suggested his blond tuft of hair resembled the U.S. president.

video:video.video:title: Scientists create walking robot fish to study evolutionary leap
video:video.video:description: How did the first ancient fish leave the water and learn to walk on land millions of years ago? Scientists think they have finally found an answer, and they used a robot to prove it.

video:video.video:title: Oz: Trump keeps getting checkups because 'he likes the results'
video:video.video:description: Dr. Mehmet Oz called President Donald Trump’s health “spectacular” and suggested the president keeps getting checkups because “he likes the results.”

video:video.video:title: Lithgow gets candid about his Tony nomination
video:video.video:description: Tony nominee John Lithgow says he wasn't surprised to earn a nomination for his portrayal of Roald Dahl in "Giant," calling it the kind of role that attracts attention.

video:video.video:title: 'Name one proven fact': Putin asks for proof of allegations of Russian illegal activity in Europe
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin held a meeting Thursday with heads of foreign media news agencies on the sidelines of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum.

video:video.video:title: Artist suing FIFA over destruction of Dallas whale mural before World Cup
video:video.video:description: The artist who painted a giant mural on a building in downtown Dallas of life-sized swimming whales has filed a $25 million lawsuit against soccer's international governing body and others, saying they illegally painted over his work to promote the city's upcoming World Cup matches.

video:video.video:title: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass advances to a runoff in her bid for reelection
video:video.video:description: After a tough first term framed by the most destructive wildfire in city history and an ongoing struggle with widespread homelessness, Bass advanced to a November runoff Tuesday as she fights to stay in City Hall against challengers from both ends of the political spectrum.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo receives royal welcome as he kicks off his six-day visit to Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV received a royal welcome from King Felipe and Queen Leticia as he kicked off a six-day visit to Spain. (AP video shot by Silvia Stellacci)

video:video.video:title: Hegseth invokes immigration and ‘invasion’ in D-Day speech in France
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech on Saturday to appear to link immigration by sea to the wartime liberation of Europe, warning that the freedom won by Allied troops could prove temporary if leaders failed to defend it.

video:video.video:title: Trump taps Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence to replace Tulsi Gabbard
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has tapped Bill Pulte, head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, to be the acting director of national intelligence.

video:video.video:title: 'The last straw': South African wheat farmers feel squeeze of Iran war
video:video.video:description: Farmers around the world are feeling the squeeze of the Iran war. Gas prices have shot up and fertilizer supplies have waned due to Tehran’s near shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for U.S. and Israeli bombing.

video:video.video:title: House approves war powers resolution to halt military action against Iran
video:video.video:description: Wednesday's action defied President Donald Trump as a handful of Republicans joined with Democrats to end the three-month-long war.

video:video.video:title: Relatives mourn Palestinian victims of overnight Israeli airstrikes on Gaza City
video:video.video:description: Nine Palestinians died in at least four separate Israeli strikes in Gaza City, according to Shifa Hospital, which received the bodies.

video:video.video:title: Iran and US exchange fire in Persian Gulf putting nominal ceasefire under further strain
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Tuesday that Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain that failed or were shot down, and that the U.S. launched strikes on an Iranian facility in response.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says Russia has 'once again chosen war'
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday said Russia "has once again chosen war." Russian leader Vladimir Putin rejected a proposal by Zelenskyy for a face-to-face meeting on ending the 4-year-old conflict, saying he sees “no point” in it.

video:video.video:title: Traders face big losses after Uganda closes Congo border over Ebola contagion fears
video:video.video:description: Traders are facing big losses after Uganda closed its border with the Democratic Republic of the Congo over Ebola contagion fears. (AP Video by Patrick Onen)

video:video.video:title: Spanish Catholic church reckons with long-standing cases of sexual abuse
video:video.video:description: Spain’s long-delayed reckoning with sexual abuse within the Catholic Church entered a new phase this year with the launch of a reparations program for cases in which accused clergy members have died and whose alleged crimes are too old to be prosecuted.

video:video.video:title: Armed clashes erupt in Somalia capital ahead of planned anti-government rally
video:video.video:description: No official casualty figures were immediately available from the violence that prompted calls for restraint from the United Nations and the United States as the government and opposition traded blame for the violence.

video:video.video:title: Javier Bardem unleashed in new 'Cape Fear'
video:video.video:description: Stars Javier Bardem, Amy Adams and Patrick Wilson explain how modern tech and family are at the heart of Apple TV's new 10-part series "Cape Fear," based on the 1962 and 1991 movies of the same name.

video:video.video:title: Footage from a boat captures Blue Origin rocket exploding on the launch pad
video:video.video:description: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad, shaking nearby homes and briefly painting the sky orange.

video:video.video:title: Ike Barinholtz on playing Elon Musk: 'After spending time in that world, I want out'
video:video.video:description: Ike Barinholtz, who portrays Elon Musk in director Luca Guadagnino's upcoming film "Artificial," said that immersing himself in Musk's world left him eager to move on: "After spending time in that world, I want out."

video:video.video:title: Israeli and Lebanese officials confirm truce agreement after US-mediated talks in Washington
video:video.video:description: Ambassadors from Israel and Lebanon said the agreement includes the creation of a number of “pilot” security zones inside Lebanon from which Hezbollah militants would be banned.

video:video.video:title: New trials ordered for two paramedics in the death of Elijah McClain
video:video.video:description: A Colorado court has ordered new trials for two paramedics found guilty in the death of Elijah McClain, almost seven years after the Black man was pinned down by police and injected with a fatal dose of ketamine.

video:video.video:title: Thousands visit University of Michigan arboretum for yearly peony peek
video:video.video:description: Visitors are making the annual visit to the University of Michigan to see and smell one of the world’s premier collections of herbaceous peonies.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans WWII Museum honors Rosie the Riveters for 82nd D-Day anniversary
video:video.video:description: For the 82nd anniversary of D-Day, New Orleans' World War II Museum features 30 Rosie the Riveters; women civilian war workers who took on demanding roles typically reserved for men during WWII.

video:video.video:title: Duet with Madonna for Barinholtz?
video:video.video:description: Ike Barinholtz discusses Madonna’s cameo in the upcoming second season of “The Studio."

video:video.video:title: Jack White: Musician turned artist
video:video.video:description: Musician Jack White explains why now is the perfect time for him to launch his debut art show in London.

video:video.video:title: Putin says it is 'far too early' to talk about whether he will run for another term as president
video:video.video:description: Putin claimed he was yet to consider whether to run for another term that would keep him in office until 2036, when he'll be 83.

video:video.video:title: Peruvian shamans perform ritual of sound and smoke to bless candidates ahead of presidential runoff
video:video.video:description: Peruvian shamans gathered on Monday to perform a ritual for the two contenders vying for the country's presidency.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 5th: Senate votes to provide $70B in new funding for DHS; U.S. economy adds 172,000 jobs in May; Virginia man sentenced to life for double-murder scheme; Putin rebuffs Zelenskyy over face-to-face meeting.

video:video.video:title: Trump says war with Iran will end 'one way or the other' after criticizing Democrats
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump reiterated his confidence that the war in Iran will soon come to an end and gas prices will fall.

video:video.video:title: In Brazil’s Cerrado region, Indigenous fire practices reshape wildfire strategy
video:video.video:description: The Xerente people in northern Tocantins state have mastered controlled burning to manage the land. Their expertise is being incorporated into Brazil's public policy as authorities combine scientific research with traditional knowledge.

video:video.video:title: Sherpa guide missing on Everest for days rescued after crawling toward base camp
video:video.video:description: A Sherpa guide was found crawling to base camp on Mount Everest a week after he went missing, and was reunited with his family who had given up hope he would return.

video:video.video:title: US Defence Secretary Hegseth at D-Day ceremony in France
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth laid tributes at a memorial in France on Saturday as he attended a ceremony marking 82 years since the D-Day landings.

video:video.video:title: Xi's trip to North Korea will be the latest step by China to reinforce its close ties with its neighbour
video:video.video:description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week, both countries have announced, in what will be his first visit in nearly seven years.

video:video.video:title: With rising crime on their minds, Peruvians vote for president yet again
video:video.video:description: Peruvians took to the polls on Sunday to choose between two presidential hopefuls with starkly different views. (AP video shot by: Cesar Barreto)

video:video.video:title: No J.Lo, then no 'Office Romance' romcom says Brett Goldstein
video:video.video:description: Actor and writer Brett Goldstein says "Office Romance," his new romcom with Jennifer Lopez, was written with the pop icon in mind and would not have been created if she hadn't signed on.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim tours missile factory as his sister says no to denuclearisation
video:video.video:description: North Korean state media has shown leader Kim Jong Un visiting a missile production facility and calling for more than doubling the country's missile production capacity.

video:video.video:title: 7.8 magnitude earthquake shakes part of southern Philippines
video:video.video:description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake has shaken part of the southern Philippines, and a tsunami was possible on some regional coasts.

video:video.video:title: Armenia prepares for an election that could reshape ties with Moscow and the West
video:video.video:description: Armenia's parliamentary elections Sunday will focus on its geopolitical future as Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan seeks closer ties with the EU and the U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, June 4th: U.S. consulate in Hong Kong commemorates Tiananmen anniversary with lit candles in windows; Nursing home fire kills at least 12 in Sri Lanka; Israeli drone strikes in Lebanon; Modi and Delcy Rodriguez meet in New Dehli.

video:video.video:title: A behind the scenes look at the FBI's Joint Operations Center in NYC ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: The World Cup, a 48-team, 104-match behemoth kicking off next week in 16 cities in the U.S., Mexico and Canada, presents an unprecedented security challenge.

video:video.video:title: Why Leo's first encyclical went viral and how it speaks to his papal approach
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has issued a manifesto calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence. His first encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” has sparked widespread online reactions, with many young people praising his willingness to address AI's rapid rise.

video:video.video:title: Haiti World Cup squad member leaves Port-au-Prince to join teammates after being granted US visa
video:video.video:description: Supporters gathered around Woodensky Pierre as he arrived at the airport in Port-au-Prince on Tuesday, telling The AP that he had “a happy heart.”

video:video.video:title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani skips annual parade celebrating Israel but pledges big police presence
video:video.video:description: Crowds of people lined the streets in New York on Sunday to attend an annual parade honoring Israel.

video:video.video:title: A prayer book, a secret bunker, tunnel and a Star of David tell a story of Jewish resistance in a Polish town
video:video.video:description: A worn prayer book, a secret bunker, and an underground tunnel used as a hideout by Jews resisting the Nazis, are among rare findings in a house in Bedzin, in southern Poland.

video:video.video:title: Two arrested in Italy after farm workers killed in apparent torching of car
video:video.video:description: Italian prosecutors were continuing an investigation on Wednesday following the arrests of two people accused in the deaths of four migrant farmworkers who were burned alive in a car.

video:video.video:title: Malaysia enforces social media ban for children under 16
video:video.video:description: Malaysia on Monday began enforcing rules barring millions of children younger than 16 from having social media accounts, joining a global effort to tighten online safety protections for young users.

video:video.video:title: Pope says he’s competing with another VIP in Madrid this weekend: Puerto Rican sensation Bad Bunny
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV acknowledged on Saturday that he was competing with another VIP in Madrid this weekend.

video:video.video:title: Death Cab for Cutie's new album is about grief — and it's for the fans
video:video.video:description: "The fans are always right." Ben Gibbard focused on fan response when crafting songs for Death Cab for Cutie's 11th studio album, “I Built You a Tower.”

video:video.video:title: What to know as the House rebukes Trump with vote to limit war powers
video:video.video:description: The House for the first time Wednesday approved a war powers resolution that would halt the U.S. military action against Iran, defying President Donald Trump as a handful of Republicans joined with Democrats to end the three-month-long conflict.
